understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by positioning the selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied simultaneously by a selectable piece; once a level reports WIN, you proceed until finishing the final level to win the whole run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (up/down/left/right) move the currently selected piece in 3-pixel grid steps (x increases to the right, y increases downward). Moves can be blocked by walls or other objects, though the movement_blocked flags are only hints and don’t guarantee failure. Some adjacent objects (blocks) can be pushed if immediately next to the selected piece in the movement direction, shifting along with the piece. When multiple selectables exist, control is switched using CLICK on the other selectable’s exact pixel position as listed in the state; the selected piece id/position updates accordingly.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear enclosed by walls on all four sides; test if the right wall is actually passable despite the blocked hint.
history.reasoning: We appear enclosed by walls on all four sides; test if the right wall is actually passable despite the blocked hint.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent walls but movement hints can be misleading; probe right to see if there’s a pass-through or push interaction.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent walls but movement hints can be misleading; probe right to see if there’s a pass-through or push interaction.

history.summary: Turn 3: CLICK | We’re boxed in; switch control to the adjacent selectable on the right to test movement from that position.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in; switch control to the adjacent selectable on the right to test movement from that position.

history.summary: Turn 4: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent walls, but we need to probe for any non-blocking boundary behavior; try pushing into the right wall to see if it slides/teleports or allows passage.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent walls, but we need to probe for any non-blocking boundary behavior; try pushing into the right wall to see if it slides/teleports or allows passage.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_LEFT | We’re boxed in by walls; try moving left to test if that wall boundary allows passing or swapping with the other selectable.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls; try moving left to test if that wall boundary allows passing or swapping with the other selectable.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_LEFT | We’re enclosed by walls on all four sides, so the only interaction possible is pushing/switching with the adjacent selectable to the left; attempt to move into it to see if it can be pushed or swapped to open space.
history.reasoning: We’re enclosed by walls on all four sides, so the only interaction possible is pushing/switching with the adjacent selectable to the left; attempt to move into it to see if it can be pushed or swapped to open space.

history.summary: Turn 7: CLICK | We’re boxed in by walls on all sides, so switch back to the other selectable to reposition it toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on all sides, so switch back to the other selectable to reposition it toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re boxed in by walls, so test the only plausible escape by trying to move into the adjacent wall on the right (some boundaries may behave oddly).
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls, so test the only plausible escape by trying to move into the adjacent wall on the right (some boundaries may behave oddly).

history.summary: Turn 9: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the adjacent wall is actually passable/acts like a door despite the blocked hint, to escape the boxed-in area.
history.reasoning: Test whether the adjacent wall is actually passable/acts like a door despite the blocked hint, to escape the boxed-in area.

history.summary: Turn 10: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear enclosed, but sometimes moving into a wall triggers a wrap/clip; test pushing right against the adjacent wall to see if it teleports us into the open area near the goal.
history.reasoning: We appear enclosed, but sometimes moving into a wall triggers a wrap/clip; test pushing right against the adjacent wall to see if it teleports us into the open area near the goal.

history.summary: Turn 11: MOVE_RIGHT | Try moving right despite the blocked hint to probe for any wrap/teleport behavior along this wall boundary.
history.reasoning: Try moving right despite the blocked hint to probe for any wrap/teleport behavior along this wall boundary.

history.summary: Turn 12: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re enclosed by walls on all four sides; try moving right to test if the heuristic ‘blocked’ still allows a push/slide interaction at the boundary.
history.reasoning: We’re enclosed by walls on all four sides; try moving right to test if the heuristic ‘blocked’ still allows a push/slide interaction at the boundary.

history.summary: Turn 13: MOVE_RIGHT | The other selectable is immediately to the right; try pushing it right to make progress toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: The other selectable is immediately to the right; try pushing it right to make progress toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 14: CLICK | We’re boxed in; switch to the other selectable at [33,21] which is nearer the goal area to attempt reaching the + tiles.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in; switch to the other selectable at [33,21] which is nearer the goal area to attempt reaching the + tiles.

history.summary: Turn 15: MOVE_RIGHT | Move onto the goal area to satisfy one of the required goal occupations; right is adjacent to the goal region.
history.reasoning: Move onto the goal area to satisfy one of the required goal occupations; right is adjacent to the goal region.

history.summary: Turn 16: MOVE_UP | We need the selected piece to occupy the single goal tile; moving up should place it onto the goal area above.
history.reasoning: We need the selected piece to occupy the single goal tile; moving up should place it onto the goal area above.
